evidence_notes: The manager goal, success/stop/boundary contract, checkpoint refresh, compact handoff, durable two-worker topology, complete write ownership, subagent-first policies, fresh verifier packet, integration/validator plan, and conservative closeout are all explicit.
It makes no thread, command, validation, registration, default-route, owner-approval, or readiness claim and keeps all refs synthetic/public-safe.

It correctly retains the declared manager, refreshes a bounded handoff, separates non-overlapping durable lanes, supplies worker/verifier packets, requires fresh non-implementer acceptance, and preserves no-execution/no-registration/default-route boundaries.
Verifier result-shape detail and subagent posture are less rigorous than Sol but remain semantically usable under the frozen gate.
Binds manager goal, checkpoint, two workers, complete packets, and fresh verifier.
Preserves subagent-first posture, independence, validator gaps, conservative ceilings, and no unsupported execution/registration/default-route/readiness claims.
